task_completion.description: Draw the 2D half-profile of the stepped shaft
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 2D sketch of a closed polygon resembling an L-shape or stacked rectangles (representing the half-profile of a stepped shaft) is visible in the SolveSpace workspace.

task_completion.description: Apply correct dimensions to the 2D profile
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dimensional constraints are visible on the sketch matching the coordinates: base width of 15, lower step height of 5, upper section width of 8, and total height of 25.

task_completion.description: Revolve the profile 360 degrees around the vertical axis
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 2D sketch is transformed into a 3D solid stepped cylindrical shaft. The revolution should be a full 360 degrees around the vertical (Y) axis.
A 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog box showing the filename 'stepped_shaft.slvs' in the '~/Documents/SolveSpace/' directory, or the main window title bar displaying 'stepped_shaft.slvs'.

task_completion.description: Export the 3D model as an STL triangle mesh
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export' dialog box or menu action showing the filename 'stepped_shaft.stl' being saved to the '~/Documents/SolveSpace/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace GUI to model the shaft.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the SolveSpace application interface being actively used (e.g., clicking tools, drawing lines, applying constraints) rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The model was created step-by-step rather than loaded from an existing file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the progression from an empty workspace to a 2D sketch, and finally to a 3D revolved solid.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor or script to generate the output files.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) or python scripts are seen generating the .slvs or .stl file contents directly.
